title: Honeybourne area Whitley Memorial
memorial_type: Rough hewn stone of remembrance with plaque

external_narratives.summary: Traces of War documents a memorial at Broadway, Worcestershire to a Whitley bomber from 24 OTU that crashed on 2 June 1943 at 14:15 hours during operational training. Five crew members were killed: Flt/Sgt H.G. Hagen (pilot), Sgt R.S. Phillips (navigator), Flt/Sgt D.H. Kelly, Sgt D.A. Marriott, and Sgt G.E. Ekins.

The Wartime Memories Project documents RAF Honeybourne as the base for No. 24 OTU during WW2. The unit used Whitley bombers for training and suffered numerous losses in the surrounding countryside, including the Z2639 incident.
key_details: RAF Honeybourne documented as 24 OTU base
Multiple training losses recorded in area

external_narratives.summary: Comprehensive record of personnel losses from No. 24 Operational Training Unit at Honeybourne, documenting the high attrition rate among training crews flying aging Whitley bombers in the Worcestershire and Warwickshire area.
key_details: Comprehensive personnel loss record for 24 OTU

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon.komoot.io — Honeybourne Worcestershire near Evesham at 52.095 -1.832
Current 52.154 -1.633 ~17km NE — actually Warwickshire not Worcestershire
location_confidence.verification_notes: CORRECTION. Current coords (52.154, -1.633) ~17km NE near Southam/Leamington in wrong county (Warwickshire). Honeybourne is in Worcestershire near Evesham at 52.095, -1.832.
